1. Overview 
 
The aim of this document is defining the TES platfo rm structures for supporting TES pipings in the 
central plant of Solana Generation Station. 
 
2. Description 
 
There are three TES platform structures on the cent ral plant as steel frame structures. The locations 
of these structures are shown in the image below: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Image Image Image Image 11 11: Location of the structures : Location of the structures : Location of the structures : Location of the structures

Wind Loads: Wind Loads: Wind Loads: Wind Loads: 
 
The wind loads have been calculated according to IB C-2006 and ASCE7-05 standard. The wind 
load has been applied to both the structure (profil es of the platforms where metal grilled floor) and 
columns, as well as supports in the profiles of the structure of the equipment as a result reactions. 
 
The load calculations are summarized below:

According to Section 6.5.13.2. of ASCE-7-05, the ne t design pressure for the main wind-force 
resisting systems of monoslope shall be determined by the following equation: 
 
p=q zGC N 
where: 
G = 0.85 (gust effect factor Section 6.5.8, roof angle 0º-6º) 
C
N= Net pressure coefficient determined from Figs. 6-19-A. 
 
 CC CCNpressure Npressure Npressure Npressure PP PPpressure pressure pressure pressure CC CCNsuction Nsuction Nsuction Nsuction PP PPsuction suction suction suction 
Zone 1 Zone 1 Zone 1 Zone 1 1.2 23.36 -1.1 21.42 
Zone 2 Zone 2 Zone 2 Zone 2 1.2 23.36 -1.1 21.42 
Zone 3 Zone 3 Zone 3 Zone 3 1.2 23.36 -1.1 21.42 
 
The two surface loads (Ppressure, Psuction), which apply throughout the floor of the structure that 
has steel grilled floor, are imcompatibles hypothes is and they can be combined with other other 
wind hypothesis. 
 
In every column, there are lineal loads for every w ind hypothesis. They are lateral and horizontal 
loads. These loads are of 27.36 lbs/ft in every col umn and every direction x and y, considering a 
coefficient of 1.2.

The earthquake loads have been calculated according to IBC2006 and ASCE7-05 standard. These 
loads are calculated automatically by calculation s oftware Metal 3D 2012.d, license 97722. The 
parameters for the calculation are: 
 
1.- Seismic importance factor, I=1.25 (table 11.5-1 from ASCE 7.05) 
 Occupancy category: III (table 1604.5 from IBC) 
2.- Ss=0.23006 
 S1=0.08845. 
 From USGS National Seismic Hazard Maps 2002. 
3.- Site class: D (according to the geo-report). 
4.- Spectral response coefficient (chapter 11.4.3 from ASCE 7.05): 
 SDS=0.245 
 SD1=0.142 (for site class D) 
5.- Seismic design category (tables 11.6-1 and 11.6-2): 
 Short period: SDC B. 
 1-sec period: SDC C. 
6.- Basic seismic force resisting system: 
 H. Steel system not specifically detailed for seismic resistance (table 12.2-1). 
7.- Design base shear (chapter 12.8 ASCE 7.05): 
 V=CsxW=0.102x1740kips=177kips. 
8.- Seismic response coefficient: 
 Cs=0.245/(3/1.25)=0.102. 
9.- Response modification factor (table 12.2-1): 
 R=3 
10.- Analysis procedure used: Modal Response Spectrum Analysis. 
 
There are some accidental loads added in some profi les due to reactions because of earthquake 
loads.

According to the manufacturer's explanation what it is shown below. Furthermore, everything is 
indicated in manufacturer’s drawings in Annex H. As summary: 
 
• Each gantry beam have the structural capacity to be supported just from the 8 places.The support 
locations indicate relate to Gridline D,E,G and H. 
 
• There are a packing which it is required to ensure that the connection pin located in the center of 
the 90 grades long Gantry beams is clear of platform steelwork.This packing will probably be from 
W10 beams suitably stiffened and will be securely a ttached to the bottom flange of gantry beam 
but not connected to platform members. These packer s will in addition act as load transfer blocks 
as they will be located directly over W18X175. It i s assumed that top of platform steelwork will be 
suitable shimmed by others to provide a series of level support points. 
 
• There will be no physical connection between our be ams (packers) and the structure so meaning 
that we exert vertical downward loads only. The gantry beams will be free to deflect upwards. 
 
 
The results of model considering loads of transport and erection of Exchangers are indicated in 
section “b” in Annex B.

5.4 Design process 
 
Determination of calculation efforts and dimensioni ng of steel frame TES platform structure are done w ith 
the help of software Metal 3D 2012.d, license 97722. 
 
Structures are designed according to the following maximum drifts: 
 
• Deflection: 1/360 of the span for floors 
 1/240 for roof members 
• Maximun drift for structures without bracings: Hei ght/300 
• Maximun drift for structures with bracings: Height /600

checking lists for each joint are provided in annex C.

Annex B. Fracture U.L.S. checks 
 
References: 
N: Axial force (lbs) 
Vy: Shear force in the local Y axis of the bar. (lbs) 
Vz: Shear force in the local Z axis of the bar. (lbs) 
Mt: Torsional moment (lbs·ft) 
My: Bending moment in the 'XZ' plane (section rotation with respect to the local 'Y' axis of the 
bar). (lbs·ft) 
Mz: Bending moment in the 'XY' plane (section rotation with respect to the local 'Z' axis of the 
bar). (lbs·ft) 
 
The indicated forces are those corresponding to the worst case combination, in other words, that 
requiring the maximum resistance of the section. 
 
Origin of worst case loads: 
− G: Only gravitational 
− GW: Gravity loads + wind 
− GE: Gravity loads + earthquake 
− GWE: Gravity loads + wind + earthquake 
 
η: Resistance usage. The bar complies with the code resistance conditions if h £ 100 %. 
 
The 3D structural model with the labels of the nodes can be seen on next page. 
Each bar check can be seen on section “a” as well, where each bar is described by the nodes it 
joins.

J.1 Salt heaters. (Plan 4521-PLN-AGE-58-54-2014, details 3, 4, 5 and 6) 
 
The loads coming from the heaters supports are applied to the beams under them. The calculations chacks 
are avalaible in section “a” in Annex B. The sets of beams under each heater are as follow: 
 
1.- Heater South-East 
 N216/N217, N897/898, N888/N887 and N192/N193. 
2.- Heater South-West 
 N901/N900, N141/N143, N917/N918, N158/N159, N859/N860 and N170/N171. 
3.- Heater North-East 
 N184/N185, N790/789, N196/N197, N792/N791, N208/N209 and N794/N793. 
4.- Heater North-West 
 N160/N161, N782/N781, N148/N149, N908/N907, N142/N137 and N784/783. 
 
J.2 Standby heat exchanger. (Plan 4521-PLN-AGE-58-54-2014, details 7 and 8)

are avalaible in section “a” in Annex B. The sets of beam under each heater are as follow: 
 
1.- Standby South: 
 N984/N410 and N986/N409. 
2.- Standby North: 
 N988/N335 and N992/N411. 
 
 
J.3 Heat exchanger supports. (Plan 4521-PLN-AGE-58-54-2020) 
 
There are two types of supports. The both are analysed by a finit element method (FEM) with the help of 
SAP2000. 
 
J.3.1 Sliding support. Hot side: 
 This support suffers the thermal growth of the heat exchanger. So there are a gap of 3” between the 
stools and the steel base plate. There is a stainless steel plate to make the friction as low as possible. 
 A syndanio layer (a thermal isolation) is designed under the steel plate in order to avoid any possible 
high temperature in the concrete. 
 A beam is welded under the steel plate to make stronger the adherence concrete-steel. 
 The support is modeled in SAP2000, with the loads that the vendor (Alpha-Laval) provided (see 
Annex1). The tractions into the concrete are as the image shows. A steel reinforcement #4 @ 9” is designed 
to control them. 
 In the structural model the steel base plate is taken into account. It is modelized in the first row of 
shells, the row at the bottom of the model. 
 The tensile by Von Mises (see picture) is less than the yield strength.
